meaning: n.奸诈,狡猾,搞两面派,口是心非,不诚实,表里不一
examples: Malcolm believed he was guilty of duplicity in his private dealings.
We are very good at duplicity and hope each other can be perceived.
For sheer duplicity there is no one to beat - the British! May the fleas of a thousand camels infest their armpits!
Khartoum promptly accused the rebels of duplicity: talking peace but trying to keep Sudan split.
Like Obama and Sarkozy, the media blame Israel and its elected leaders for the Palestinians' duplicity.
